children.heading: .—Consultation on, and Submission of, Proposals
children.label: a .—Consultation on, and Submission of, Proposals

children.heading: .—Consultation
children.label: 1 .—Consultation
children.text: The sponsor must consult with the National Capital Memorial Advisory Commission regarding the selection of alternative sites and design concepts for the commemorative work.

children.heading: .—Submittal
children.label: 2 .—Submittal
children.text: Following consultation in accordance with clause (1), the Secretary of the Interior or the Administrator of General Services, as appropriate, must submit, on behalf of the sponsor, site and design proposals to the Commission of Fine Arts and the National Capital Planning Commission for their approval.

children.heading: .—Surroundings
children.label: 1 .—Surroundings
children.text: To the maximum extent possible, a commemorative work shall be located in surroundings that are relevant to the subject of the work.

children.label: A
children.text: it does not interfere with, or encroach on, an existing commemorative work; and

children.label: B
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, it protects open space, existing public use, and cultural and natural resources.

children.heading: .—Material
children.label: 3 .—Material
children.text: A commemorative work shall be constructed of durable material suitable to the outdoor environment.

children.heading: .—Landscape features
children.label: 4 .—Landscape features
children.text: Landscape features of commemorative works shall be compatible with the climate.

children.heading: .—Museums
children.label: 5 .—Museums
children.text: No commemorative work primarily designed as a museum may be located on lands under the jurisdiction of the Secretary in Area I or in East Potomac Park as depicted on the map referenced in section 8902(2).1So in original. Probably should be section “8902(a)(2).”1

children.heading: .—Site-specific guidelines
children.label: 6 .—Site-specific guidelines
children.text: The National Capital Planning Commission and the Commission of Fine Arts may develop such criteria or guidelines specific to each site that are mutually agreed upon to ensure that the design of the commemorative work carries out the purposes of this chapter.

children.heading: .—Acknowledgment of donor contribution
children.label: 1 .—Acknowledgment of donor contribution
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection, the Secretary of the Interior or Administrator of General Services, as applicable, may permit a sponsor to acknowledge donor contributions at the commemorative work.

children.label: i
children.text: inside an ancillary structure associated with the commemorative work; or

children.label: ii
children.text: as part of a manmade landscape feature at the commemorative work; and

children.label: B
children.text: conform to applicable National Park Service or General Services Administration guidelines for donor recognition, as applicable.

children.label: A
children.text: be limited to an appropriate statement or credit recognizing the contribution;

children.label: B
children.text: be displayed in a form in accordance with National Park Service and General Services Administration guidelines;

children.label: C
children.text: be displayed for a period of up to 10 years, with the display period to be commensurate with the level of the contribution, as determined in accordance with the plan and guidelines described in subparagraph (B);

children.label: D
children.text: be freestanding; and

children.label: i
children.text: any landscape feature at the commemorative work; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any object in a museum collection.

children.heading: .—Cost
children.label: 4 .—Cost
children.text: The sponsor shall bear all expenses related to the display of donor acknowledgments under paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Applicability
children.label: 5 .—Applicability
children.text: This subsection shall apply to any commemorative work dedicated after .January 1, 2010
